<title level="1" right="Spell 8" pfs="Standard">
[Clone Companion](/Spells.aspx?ID=885)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 96

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** your  companion 
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a duplicate of your companion, and the duplicate fights alongside its twin, mirroring the original's actions. The duplicate appears adjacent to your companion and has the same statistics.

 Each time you [Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) your companion, both your companion and its duplicate gain 2 actions. The duplicate always acts immediately after your companion and must use identical actions to the ones your companion used, in exactly the same order. However, it can use the actions differently, such as Striding to a different position or selecting a different target for a Strike. If the duplicate is unable to mimic an action, it performs the action without result and the action is wasted. The duplicate can't use any actions that can be used only a limited number of times per day.

 The duplicate isn't truly alive and can't be healed in any way. If the duplicate ever reaches 0 Hit Points, it is instantly destroyed and _clone companion_ immediately ends.
name: Clone Companion

The number of actions you spend Casting this Spell determines its targets, range, area, and other parameters. **(verbal)** The spell deals 1d4 sonic damage to a single enemy, with a basic Fortitude save.
**(somatic, verbal)** The spell deals 2d4 sonic damage to all creatures in a 10-foot burst, with a basic Fortitude save.
**(material, somatic, verbal)** The spell deals 2d4 sonic damage to all creatures in a 30-foot emanation, with a basic Fortitude save. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4 for the 1-action version, or 2d4 for the other versions.

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Coral Eruption](/Spells.aspx?ID=887)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 96

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** two 10-foot bursts that don't overlap

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Razor sharp coral growths erupt from the ground, transforming the landscape into a dangerous coral reef. You can create a third nonoverlapping burst if the centers of all three bursts are underwater. Creatures in the area when the spell is cast take 6d6 piercing damage and must attempt a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails its saving throw also takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The area becomes difficult terrain and hazardous terrain.

 A creature that moves through the area takes 3 piercing damage for every square of that area it moves into.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The initial piercing damage increases by 3d6, and the hazardous terrain damage increases by 1.
name: Coral Eruption

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Corrosive Body](/Spells.aspx?ID=888)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Acid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=3" />
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 97

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deities**
[Teshallas](/Deities.aspx?ID=550), [Xsistaid](/Deities.aspx?ID=423)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You exhale corrosive gas, and acidic secretions coat your skin as you transform into a being of living acid. You gain [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3) immunity. Any creature that touches you or damages you with an unarmed melee attack or non-reach melee weapon takes 3d6 acid damage, and your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks deal 1d4 additional acid damage. The first time each round that you deal acid damage to a creature in this way, you gain 3d6 temporary HP as your body digests the eroded material and transforms it into a protective acid. When the spell ends, any remaining temporary HP expires as well. Your acid spells deal one additional die of acid damage (of the same damage die the spell uses). You can cast [_acid splash_](/Spells.aspx?ID=3) as an innate spell; the splash damage affects all creatures within 15 feet instead of the normal 5 feet.

---
**Heightened (9th)** Creatures touching you take 4d6 acid damage instead of 3d6, your unarmed attacks deal 2d4 additional acid damage, and you gain 5d6 temporary HP.
name: Corrosive Body

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 98 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- You recreate your form entirely out of cosmic energy, transforming into a Large battle form. You must have enough space to expand into, or the spell is lost. When you cast this spell, choose to take on the powers of the moon or the sun. You have hands in this battle form and can use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. You can Dismiss the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of the form that you choose:
  • AC = 21 + your level. Ignore your armor check's penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 20 temporary Hit Points.
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more attacks specific to the battle form you use. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +25, and you use the listed damage. The melee attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example), and the ranged attacks are Dexterity based. If your unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +25, unless your own is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the stellar body: - **Moon** Speed 30 feet, fly 50 feet; **Melee** fist ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d4+10 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire; **Ranged** moonbeam (range 90 feet), **Damage** 2d4+10 fire; this form's fire damage is [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) for resistances, weaknesses, and the like; on a critical hit with either Strike, the target is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round. - **Sun** Speed 30 feet, fly 50 feet; **Melee** fist ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 2d6+10 fire plus 1d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Ranged** sunbeam ([range 90 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 1d6+10 fire plus 1d6 persistent fire; on a critical hit with either Strike, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round.
name: Cosmic Form

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 98 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- You infuse yourself with the corrupting death and pure malevolence of [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12), transforming into a Large daemon battle form. You must have enough space to expand into or the spell is lost. When you cast this spell you choose either lacridaemon, [leukodaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2893), [meladaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=593), or [venedaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2892). The battle form is Medium if you choose lacridaemon or venedaemon. While in this form, you gain the [daemon](/Traits.aspx?ID=213) and [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) traits. You have hands in this battle form and can use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of the form that you choose:
  • AC = 21 + your level. Ignore your armor check's penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 15 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) and [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 5 to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517).
  • A +2 status bonus to all saving throws against [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effects.
  • [Resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126).
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more attacks specific to the battle form you use. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +21, and you use the listed damage. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your unarmed attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +23, unless your own modifier is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the type of Daemon you choose: - **Lacridaemon** Speed 25 feet, fly 40 feet; resistance 10 to cold; **Melee** jaws ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d8+12 piercing plus 1d6 cold and 1d4 poison; **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d6+12 slashing plus 1d6 cold. - **[Leukodaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2893)** Speed 25 feet, fly 40 feet; +2 status bonus to saves against [diseases](/Traits.aspx?ID=578); **Melee** jaws ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing; **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing; **Ranged** composite longbow ([deadly 1d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=570), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [propulsive](/Traits.aspx?ID=677), [range increment 100 feet](/Rules.aspx?ID=2195), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [volley](/Traits.aspx?ID=730)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing. - **[Meladaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=593)** Speed 25 feet, fly 40 feet; **Melee** jaws ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d10+12 piercing; **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510)), **Damage** 1d6+12 slashing plus 1d6 void, and you can spend an action after a hit to attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target. - **[Venedaemon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2892)** Speed 25 feet; sense [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644) items and active spells within 60 feet as an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=2407); **Melee** tentacle ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning.

The creature must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) and [enfeebled 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) for 1 round.
**Failure** The target is fatigued and enfeebled 1 for the duration.
**Critical Failure** The target is fatigued and enfeebled 2 for the duration. The fatigued condition doesn't end when the spell's duration does (but can still be removed with a night's rest or by similar means). --- **Heightened (6th)** You can target up to 10 creatures.

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Deity's Strike](/Spells.aspx?ID=892)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Force" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=75" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 99

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

**Requirements** You have a deity.
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

**Target** 1 creature (see text)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

A manifested version of your deity's favored weapon appears in the air above and attacks the target, using your spell attack roll. On a hit, the weapon deals 7d12 force damage, or double damage on a critical hit.

 After the attack, whether it succeeds or fails, divine energy bursts from the weapon in a shock wave. It emits in a 10-foot-wide, 30-foot-long line starting from the target and continuing in a direction you choose. Choose an alignment your deity has (chaotic, evil, good, or lawful). Your target and any other creatures within the area of the spell take 5d6 damage of the chosen alignment type, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The spell gains the trait of the alignment you choose. If your deity is true neutral, the weapon doesn't create a shock wave.

---
**Heightened (9th)** The force damage increases by 1d12 and the alignment damage increases by 1d6.
name: Deity's Strike

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 99 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- You corrupt yourself with the sin of the [Outer Rifts](/Planes.aspx?ID=45), transforming into a Medium demon battle form. When you cast this spell, choose [abrikandilu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1110), [brimorak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2896), [kithangian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2457), or [vloriak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2727). If you choose kithangian, the battle form is Large and you must have enough space to expand into or the spell is lost. While in this form, you gain the [demon](/Traits.aspx?ID=42) and [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) traits. You have hands in this battle form and can use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of the form that you choose:
  • AC = 20 + your level. Ignore your armor check's penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 30 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321), [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317) 5 to [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375), and weakness 5 to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517).
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more attacks specific to the battle form you use. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +22, and you use the listed damage. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your attack modifier is higher for the given unarmed attack or weapon, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +23, unless your own modifier is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the type of demon you choose: - **[Abrikandilu](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1110)** Speed 25 feet; **Melee** jaws ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 piercing, and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61) 1 for 1 minute on a critical hit; **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 slashing; **Ranged** hurled debris ([range increment 20 feet](/Rules.aspx?ID=2195), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+6 bludgeoning. - **[Brimorak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2896)** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** flaming sword ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+10 slashing plus 1d6 fire; **Melee** hoof ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d4+10 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire. - **[Kithangian](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2457)** Speed 30 feet; **Melee** pincer ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d12+12 slashing, and you can spend an action after a hit to attempt to [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=45) the target; **Melee** stinger ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d8+12 piercing; all melee Strikes deal 2d6 additional precision damage to [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=531). - **[Vloriak](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2727)** Speed 25 feet; [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to [acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=524); **Melee** claw ([unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d6+12 piercing plus 1d6 spirit; **Melee** tongue ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d6+12 acid plus 1d6 spirit.
name: Demon Form

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 99 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- You bind yourself to the power of [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18), transforming into a Medium [devil](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=30) battle form. When you cast this spell, choose [coarti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2907), [levaloch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1114), [sarglagon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2908), or [vordine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2906). If you choose levaloch or sarglagon, the battle form is Large and you must have enough space to expand into or the spell is lost. While in this form you gain the [devil](/Traits.aspx?ID=44) and [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) traits. You have hands in this battle form and can use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. You can [Dismiss](/Actions.aspx?ID=2311) the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of the form that you choose:
  • AC = 22 + your level. Ignore your armor check's penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321), [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 5 to physical damage (except [silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379)), and weakness 5 to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517).
  • Resistance 10 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72).
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more attacks specific to the battle form you use. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +20, and you use the listed damage. These attacks are Strength based (for the purpose of the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) condition, for example). If your attack modifier is higher for the given unarmed attack or weapon, you can use it instead.
  • [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +23, unless your own modifier is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the type of devil you choose: - **[Coarti](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2907)** Speed 25 feet, fly 40 feet; **Melee** morningstar ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 2d6+12 bludgeoning plus 1d6 spirit; **Melee** wing ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=724)), **Damage** 1d6+12 bludgeoning plus 1d6 fire. - **[Levaloch](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1114)** Speed 30 feet, climb 30 feet; +2 circumstance bonus to Fortitude DC against being [Shoved](/Actions.aspx?ID=2380) and to other saving throws to resist being [moved against your will](/Rules.aspx?ID=2364); Melee trident ([magical](/Rules.aspx?ID=3122), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), Damage 2d8+10 piercing; **Ranged** trident ([magical](/Rules.aspx?ID=3122), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), Damage 2d8+10 piercing; all melee Strikes deal 2d6 additional precision damage to [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=61), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71), [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81), or [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) creatures. - **[Sarglagon](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2908)** Speed 25 feet, fly 25 feet, swim 30 feet; **Melee** fangs ([magical](/Rules.aspx?ID=3122), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d12+10 piercing; **Melee** tentacle arm ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Rules.aspx?ID=3122), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d8+10 bludgeoning plus 1d6 poison. - **[Vordine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2906)** Speed 25 feet; **Melee** trident ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), unholy), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing; Melee hoof ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 1d4+12 plus 1d6 fire; **Melee** whip ([disarm](/Traits.aspx?ID=577), [magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=661), [reach 10 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=684), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=716), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d4+10 slashing; **Ranged** trident ([magical](/Traits.aspx?ID=644), [thrown 20 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=711), [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521)), **Damage** 2d8+12 piercing.
name: Devil Form

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 100 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Deities** [Titivilus](/Deities.aspx?ID=530), [Velgaas](/Deities.aspx?ID=601) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** varies --- You bombard a target with distracting auditory illusions, surrounding them with a tumultuous cacophony of overlapping speech, whispers, screams, and muttering. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16), takes a –2 status penalty to purely [auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16) Perception checks, and must succeed at a DC 5 flat check to use auditory abilities or verbal spell components. Similarly, a creature who attempts to affect the target with an auditory effect must succeed at a DC 5 flat check or the attempt fails. The duration depends on the target's Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The duration is 1 round.
**Failure** The duration is 3 rounds.
**Critical Failure** The duration is 1 minute. --- **Heightened (7th)** You can target up to five creatures.
name: Distracting Chatter

<title level="1" right="Spell 8" pfs="Standard">
[Divine Armageddon](/Spells.aspx?ID=896)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Negative" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=118" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 100

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 60-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

You call forth a divine cataclysm from your deity, destroying living and [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) creatures in the area alike. Creatures in the area take 10d6 negative damage and 10d6 alignment damage (good, evil, lawful, or chaotic), chosen from among the alignments your deity has. If your deity is true neutral, increase the negative damage by 4d6 instead of dealing alignment damage. A creature harmed by positive damage, such as one with [negative healing](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=42), takes positive damage instead of negative damage from this spell.

 You can't cast this spell if you don't have a deity. This spell gains the trait corresponding to the alignment damage dealt.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6 negative damage, 1d6 alignment damage, and 1d6 additional negative and positive damage for a true neutral deity.
name: Divine Armageddon

You deal 1d10 mental damage to the creature and cause it to take a –1 status penalty to attack rolls against creatures other than you. The creature must attempt a Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.

**Failure** The target takes full damage and the penalty.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage, and the status penalty is –2. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d10.

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 creature that is either taller than you or higher In the air than you
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Reflex

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Calling out to the skies, you summon a bolt of lightning to strike through a foe above you and down into your weapon or your body, charging it with electrical power. You must hold your weapon or an empty hand aloft as part of this spell's somatic component. When you do, a bolt of lightning descends from a storm cloud in the air above your and through the target, dealing 3d12 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save. For the rest of the spell's duration, your first Strike each round with the weapon you held aloft (or with your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks if you held an empty hand aloft) deals an additional 1d12 electricity damage.

 The spell creates its own storm cloud if necessary, so you can cast _draw the lightning_ anywhere, even underground. If _draw the lightning_ is cast outside under a cloudy or stormy sky, increase the bolt's damage by 2d12.

---
**Heightened (8th)** The damage dealt to the initial target increases by 4d12, and the additional damage dealt on the first Strike each turn increases by 1d12.
name: Draw the Lightning

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Echoing Weapon](/Spells.aspx?ID=899)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 101

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 weapon
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You channel magical energy into the target weapon, and the air around it faintly hums each time you strike a blow, as the impact is absorbed into the weapon. If a creature is wielding the weapon at the end of its turn, the weapon discharges a burst of sound targeting one creature adjacent to the wielder (if any). The sonic damage this deals is equal to the number of successful Strikes with the target weapon that the wielder made that turn (to a maximum of 4 sonic damage if the wielder hits with four Strikes).

---
**Heightened (+2)** The sonic damage increases by 1 per Strike (and the maximum damage increases by 4).
name: Echoing Weapon

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 101 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- Drawing upon the deepest wells of magic in an elemental plane, you transform into a spectacular elemental. You can choose between an air elemental, earth elemental, fire elemental, or water elemental. Your battle form is Gargantuan, and you must have enough space to expand into or the spell is lost. While in this form, you gain the [elemental](/Traits.aspx?ID=57) trait and the trait for the element you choose. You can Dismiss the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of which battle form you choose:
  • AC = 25 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 30 temporary Hit Points.
  • [Darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415).
  • One or more attacks specific to the battle form you choose, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +34, and you use the listed damage. These attacks are Dexterity based if you choose an air or fire elemental, or Strength based if you choose an earth or water elemental. (This distinction doesn't change the statistics, but matters for the [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13) and [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) conditions, for example). If your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attack modifier is higher, you can use it instead.
  • [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) modifier of +36 for air or fire elemental or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) modifier of +36 for earth or water elemental, unless your own modifier is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the type of elemental you choose: Secrets of Magic - **Air Elemental** fly Speed 80 feet; **High Winds** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206)) 30 feet. Air within the emanation is difficult terrain for flying creatures that don't have the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait; **Swiftness** Your movement doesn't trigger reactions; **Melee** gust (reach 25 feet), **Damage** 3d12+11 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) 10 feet; **Ranged** lightning lash ([range increment 80 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 3d12+4 electricity. - **Earth Elemental** 30 feet, burrow 20 feet; resistance 5 to physical; **Spike Stones** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) 30 feet. The area is difficult terrain and hazardous terrain. A creature without the [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55) trait that moves on the ground in the area takes 5 piercing damage for every square of that area it moves into; **Rocky Toughness** You gain 40 temporary Hit Points when you choose this form instead of 30; **Melee** fist ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+15 bludgeoning; **Ranged** rock ([range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+6 bludgeoning. - **Fire Elemental** 50 feet; immunity to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72); weakness to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) 10; **Intense Heat** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) 30 feet. A creature that enters the aura or starts its turn in the aura takes 5d6 fire damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your spell DC. A creature can take damage from the aura only once per round; **Melee** tendril ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+12 fire plus 2d6 [persistent fire](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29); **Ranged** fire mote ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+6 fire. - **Water Elemental** 40 feet, swim 80 feet; resistance 10 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72); **Vortex** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) 30 feet. Water within the aura that is part of the same body of water you occupy is difficult terrain for [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) creatures that don't have the [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165) trait; **Melee** wave ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d12+18 bludgeoning plus [Push](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=25) or Pull 10 feet; **Ranged** water spout ([range increment 60 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+6 bludgeoning. Rage of Elements - **Metal Elemental** 40 feet, fly 40 feet; resistance 10 to electricity; **Arcing Electricity** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) 30 feet. A creature that enters the aura or starts its turn in the aura takes 2d12 electricity damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save against your spell DC; a creature made of metal, with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) trait, or wearing metal armor takes a –1 circumstance penalty on this save. A creature can take damage from the aura only once per round; **Melee** blade ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192), [versatile slashing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 3d12+15 piercing; **Ranged** metal shard ([range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248), [versatile slashing](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 4d8+6 piercing. The metal Strike is Dexterity based. - **Wood Elemental** 30 feet, climb 35 feet; resistance 5 to physical; weakness to [axes](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=1) 10; **Lush Growth** ([aura](/Traits.aspx?ID=206), [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) 30 feet. Ground in the area is difficult terrain, and any time a creature in the area regains HP from a [healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=89) [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) effect, the aura grants a +5 status bonus to the healing; **Woody Toughness** You gain 40 temporary Hit Points when you choose this form instead of 30; **Melee** branch ([reach 25 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=192)), **Damage** 3d10+18 bludgeoning; **Ranged** seed ([range increment 40 feet](/Traits.aspx?ID=248)), **Damage** 4d8+6 bludgeoning. The wood Strike is Strength based.
name: Element Embodied

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 102 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Deity** [Khepri](/Deities.aspx?ID=635) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- You absorb elemental energy, which you can release as a powerful counterattack. Choose [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165). You gain resistance 5 to damage dealt by effects with the chosen elemental trait; if you choose water, you also gain resistance to [cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27) damage. As usual, if a spell has a multiple effects, such as a spell that deals both fire damage and bludgeoning damage with superheated rocks, you gain resistance to only the effects associated with the element you chose. Keep track of how much damage you have prevented with this spell. As a 2-action activity, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait and the trait matching the element you chose, you can release the stored energy. This deals damage equal to the amount you've stored (maximum 30 damage) to one enemy within 60 feet, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. The damage type depends on the element you chose: fire damage for fire; bludgeoning or cold damage for water; bludgeoning or slashing damage for air; or bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage for earth. After releasing the energy, the spell ends. If you do not use the stored energy before the spell ends, the energy is lost. Elemental Spell Changes**Source** [_Rage of Elements pg. 55_](/Sources.aspx?ID=205)
Add metal (electricity or slashing damage) and wood (bludgeoning or vitality damage) to the choices. --- **Heightened (+3)** The resistance increases by 5 and the maximum damage increases by 30.
name: Elemental Absorption

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 102 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Area** 30-foot cone **Saving Throw** [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex --- You draw elemental power from your surroundings, and combining it with your own magical energy, unleash a wave of utter destruction and swirling colors that deals 1d6 fire damage and 3d6 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines the area and other parameters. **(somatic, verbal)** The spell is a 30-foot cone.
**(material, somatic, verbal)** The spell is a 30-foot cone. On a failed saving throw, creatures are [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 5 feet away from you, and on a critical failure they are pushed 10 feet away and are knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).
**Two Rounds** If you spend 3 actions Casting the Spell, you can avoid finishing the spell and spend another 3 actions on your next turn to empower the spell even further. If you do, the spell is as 3 actions, but the area is a 60-foot cone, and for 1 round, the elements linger in the cone, racing off into the distance and making approaching you difficult, with the effects of [_gust of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=143). Elemental Spell Changes **Source** [_Rage of Elements pg. 55_](/Sources.aspx?ID=205)
You can replace the [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5) trait with the [metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505) and [wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508) traits when you cast the spell. --- **Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d6 fire and 3d6 bludgeoning, and the distance that enemies are pushed back if you spent 3 actions or 2 rounds increases by 5 feet on a failed save and 10 feet on a critical failure.
name: Elemental Annihilation Wave

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Elemental Confluence](/Spells.aspx?ID=903)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 102

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

**Deity**
[Valani](/Deities.aspx?ID=486)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Area** 10-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure four types of elementals who combine forces to form into a chaotic storm. The confluence has AC 28 and 120 Hit Points, weakness 10 to area effects, and immunity to bleed and [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126). Creatures can move through the confluence; creatures within the confluence are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the confluence are concealed to creatures within it. When you Cast or Sustain this Spell, you choose up to two different types of elementals to act.

You can swap in a metal elemental, a wood elemental, or both in place of the standard elementals.

<title level="2">Secrets of Magic</title>

- **Air** ([air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56)) The elemental looses a bolt of electricity from the confluence, dealing 4d4 electricity damage to creatures within 20 feet of the confluence, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. This doesn't affect creatures that are completely inside of the confluence.
- **Earth** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55)) The confluence gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC and resistance 10 to all physical damage (except [adamantine](/Equipment.aspx?ID=374)) for 1 round.
- **Fire** ([fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) Flames roar through the confluence, dealing 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to those partially or entirely inside the confluence.
- **Water** ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=15), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) The elemental expels a powerful jet of water. Attempt a ranged spell attack against a target within 60 feet of the confluence, using your spell attack roll modifier. This attack ignores concealment granted by the confluence, and the elemental gets a +2 circumstance bonus on the spell attack roll against a target inside the confluence. The water deals 4d6 bludgeoning damage on a hit (doubled on a critical hit). This attack doesn't count toward your multiple attack penalty.

<title level="2">Rage of Elements</title>

- **Metal** ([metal](/Traits.aspx?ID=505)) The elemental flings shards of metal through the confluence, dealing 2d6 slashing damage to those partially or entirely inside the confluence with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save. A creature that critically fails also takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
- **Wood** ([wood](/Traits.aspx?ID=508)) Roots and stumps grow, making ground in the area [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=453).
name: Elemental Confluence

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Elemental Gift](/Spells.aspx?ID=904)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 103

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

**Deity**
[Keltheald](/Deities.aspx?ID=474)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

An elemental force fills your target, granting the target the swiftness of air, the ruggedness of earth, the sting of fire, or the flexibility of water, depending on which element you choose. Choose an element when you Cast the Spell. The target gains the benefit of that element as described below, and this spell gains that trait's element.

<title level="2">Secrets of Magic</title>

- **Air** The target gains a +30-foot status bonus to its land Speed and gains a fly Speed equal to its land Speed without the status bonus.
- **Earth** While on the ground, the target gets a +2 status bonus to Fortitude and Reflex saves against effects that would [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) or [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=40) it, and to saves against effects that would attempt to knock it [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). In addition, the ground adjacent to the target is difficult terrain, and the difficult terrain moves with the target, though the target ignores this difficult terrain with its own movement.
- **Fire** The target's melee [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strikes and melee weapon Strikes deal 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) on a hit.
- **Water** The target takes on a watery sheen, gaining resistance 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) and a swim Speed equal to its land Speed.

<title level="2">Rage of Elements</title>

- **Metal** The target's Strikes using a metal weapon or an unarmed attack deal an additional 1d4 electricity damage on a hit and get a +1 status bonus to the attack roll if the target is made of metal or wearing metal armor.
- **Wood** The target gains a number of temporary Hit Points equal to the spell's rank, then gains half that number of temporary HP at the start of each of its turns.

---
**Heightened (8th)** You can target up to 5 willing creatures.
name: Elemental Gift

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 104 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Spell Lists** [Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 120 feet **Area** 20-foot radius burst **Duration** 1 minute --- Select an elemental trait: [air](/Traits.aspx?ID=5), [earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165). _Elemental zone_ gains the trait you chose. You imbue the area with the raw energy of that element, creating a zone that emits and amplifies magic of that type. The temperature might suddenly rise or fall, a storm cloud might form, and so on. Spells with the chosen elemental trait cast against creatures in the zone get a +2 status bonus to one damage type the spell deals based on the chosen trait: bludgeoning or electricity for air, bludgeoning for earth, fire for fire, and bludgeoning or cold for water. The caster chooses one type to add the bonus to if the spell deals more than one eligible type. This bonus is halved if the spell didn't use a spell slot (such as a cantrip, focus spell, or innate spell). The zone has no effect on the spell if the spell doesn't deal any damage of an eligible type. Elemental Spell Changes**Source** [_Rage of Elements pg. 55_](/Sources.aspx?ID=205)
Add metal (electricity or slashing damage) and wood (bludgeoning or vitality damage) to the choices. --- **Heightened (+2)** The status bonus increases by 1.
name: Elemental Zone

<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** your  companion  or  eidolon 
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You envenom your companion or eidolon's attacks, which drip with highly toxic venom for the spell's duration. The first time each round the target hits a creature with an unarmed Strike that deals piercing or slashing damage, that Strike deals an additional 1d8 poison damage. A creature that is damaged by this poison must attempt a Fortitude save. On a failure, it's also [clumsy 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) for 1 round.

 This spell can't target familiars.

---
**Heightened (+3)** The poison damage increases by 1d8 and the clumsy value imposed on a failed save increases by 1.
name: Envenom Companion

[Extract Poison](/Spells.aspx?ID=907)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 104

[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 poison on or in an object
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

With the lightest touch, you can siphon the poison from an  object and save it for your own later use, making an assassin's blade or politician's wine less deadly while you become all the more so. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against one poison you're aware of on or in an object you touch. If you successfully counteract the poison, you negate the object's toxicity and transfer the poison into a weapon you are holding, coating the weapon with a simple but powerful poison. On your next successful attack with that weapon before the end of your next turn, you add 1d6 poison damage per level of the poison you counteracted. On a critically failed attack roll, you lose the extracted poison from your weapon as normal.
name: Extract Poison

<title level="1" right="Spell 10" pfs="Standard">
[Fated Confrontation](/Spells.aspx?ID=908)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 105

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 willing ally and 1 enemy
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You weave the threads of fate to lead two foes into a climactic battle. The targets of this spell are the fated opponents: one of the targets of this spell must be you or a willing ally, and the other must be an enemy.

 When you Cast the Spell, the ally you targeted (or you, if you targeted yourself) may teleport to any unoccupied space of their choice within 30 feet of its fated opponent. This is a [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156) effect.

 You isolate the two targets for 1 round. During this time, fate refuses to allow either fated opponent to be affected by attacks or effects from a third party; a fated opponent could be affected by the other's [_meteor swarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=191) or its own [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) spell, but it would be entirely immune to a spell or Strike from a creature outside of the spells effects, as well including damage from a hazard or the environment. This doesn't apply to anything that was affecting the targets before you cast _fated confrontation_ (such as a spell with a duration or persistent damage the creature had before it was affected by _fated confrontation_).

 For the duration of the spell, either fated opponent can manipulate fate once on each of its turns. This allows it to either roll twice and take the higher result on an attack roll or skill check it makes against its fated opponent (a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect), or force the fated opponent to roll twice on a saving throw against one of its abilities and take the lower result (a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect).

 This spell ends if either target becomes unable to act, or if they both agree to cease hostilities.
name: Fated Confrontation

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 105 **Traditions** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Duration** 1 minute --- Channeling the mischievous fey, you transform into a fey battle form, which is Medium unless stated otherwise. When you cast this spell, choose dryad, elananx, naiad, redcap, or unicorn. If you choose unicorn, the battle form is Large and you must have space to expand or the spell is lost. While in this form, you gain the [fey](/Traits.aspx?ID=69) trait. Unless you choose elananx or unicorn, you have hands in this battle form and can use [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) actions. You can Dismiss the spell. You gain the following statistics and abilities regardless of the form that you choose:
  • AC = 19 + your level. Ignore your armor's check penalty and Speed reduction.
  • 15 temporary Hit Points and weakness 5 to cold iron.
  • [Low-light vision](/Rules.aspx?ID=416).
  • One or more [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) melee attacks specific to the battle form you choose, which are the only attacks you can Strike with. You're trained with them. Your attack modifier is +16, and you use the listed damage. These attacks are Dexterity based. If your attack modifier is higher for the given unarmed attack or weapon, you can use it instead.
  • [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) modifiers of +16, unless your own modifier is higher.
You also gain specific abilities based on the type of fey: - **[Dryad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=312)** The temporary HP gained increases to 30; Speed 25 feet; weakness 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72); **Melee** branch, **Damage** 1d12+8 bludgeoning. - **[Elananx](/Monsters.aspx?ID=176)** Speed 30 feet; resistance 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72); **Melee** jaws, **Damage** 1d6+8 piercing plus 1d6 fire; **Melee** claw ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d6+8 slashing. - **[Naiad](/Monsters.aspx?ID=311)** Speed 25 feet, swim 25 feet; resistance 5 to [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72); **Melee** aqueous fist ([water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)), **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning. - **[Redcap](/Monsters.aspx?ID=349)** Speed 40 feet; Size Small; **Melee** scythe ([deadly d10](/Traits.aspx?ID=174), [trip](/Traits.aspx?ID=196)), **Damage** 1d10+8 slashing; **Melee** boot ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [versatile B](/Traits.aspx?ID=200)), **Damage** 1d6+8 piercing. - **[Unicorn](/Monsters.aspx?ID=397)** gain the [beast](/Traits.aspx?ID=20) trait in addition to fey; Speed 40 feet; resistance 5 to [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126); Size Large; **Melee** horn, **Damage** 1d10+8 piercing plus 1 good; **Melee** hoof ([agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170)), **Damage** 1d8+8 bludgeoning; all Strikes have the effects of a [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297) property rune.
name: Fey Form

<traits>

---

You combine a blazing inferno and air currents into a fiery vortex. You can Cast this Spell only if you are outside or the ceiling is 40 feet or higher. All squares in the vortex are difficult terrain for flying creatures.

 All creatures in the area take 3d4 bludgeoning damage and 3d6 fire damage as gusts of fiery wind buffet them, with a basic Reflex save. Each time you Sustain the Spell, you can move the whirlwind up to 20 feet in a straight line. Each creature the whirlwind moves through takes the damage with a basic Reflex save. A creature can take damage from a _flame vortex_ only once per round.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The vortex deals an additional 1d4 bludgeoning damage and 1d6 fire damage.
name: Flame Vortex

<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 106

**Traditions**

**Deity**

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Sending magic deep underground, you conjure a cloud of toxic gases that swiftly degenerate into volatile fumes. The gases are invisible, requiring a successful Perception check against your spell DC to detect visually, though their acrid smell and toxic effects are clear once a creature has entered the cloud. A creature that enters the cloud or is within the cloud at the start of its turn takes 2d6 poison damage. A creature can take the poison damage from _flammable fumes_ only once per round.

 One round after you conjure the cloud, the gases loses stability and become flammable. If an open flame is brought into the cloud, or if anyone within the area uses a fire effect, the cloud detonates in a massive blaze that deals 10d6 fire damage with a basic Reflex save to all creatures within it, and the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The poison damage increases by 1d6 and the fire damage on an explosion increases by 2d6.
name: Flammable Fumes

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 106 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 60 feet **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- A Medium floating, disembodied hand made of magical force appears in an unoccupied space adjacent to you, following you across the battlefield to shield you against your foes' attacks. Each time you Sustain the Spell, the hand moves to an unoccupied space of your choice adjacent to you. When you Sustain the Spell, you can have the hand [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94) to an unoccupied space of your choice adjacent to an ally to protect that ally instead. The hand's movement does not trigger reactions based on creature movement. The hand takes space, but creatures can move through the hand freely. Successful attacks against the hand damage it, and damaging effects that can target a creature can target the hand. The hand has an AC of 25, 50 Hit Points, and can't recover HP by any means. A hit by a [_disintegrate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=76) spell or similar effect destroys the hand. The hand's actions and the cover it provides depend on what actions you take during your turn. At the end of your turn, what the hand does is determined by the first entry on this list that matches a trait from any of the actions you used on your turn. For instance, if you Step, Stride, and Sustain the Spell, you use the entry for [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions, so the hand provides standard cover.
  • **Manipulate or Attack** The hand interposes between you and your foes. It provides lesser cover (+1 circumstance bonus to AC) against all attacks that pass through the hand's square.
  • **Move** The hand provides standard cover (+2 circumstance bonus to AC, Reflex, and Stealth checks) against all attacks that pass through the hand's square.
  • **Concentrate** The hand provides greater cover (+4 circumstance bonus to AC, Reflex, and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks) against all attacks that pass through the hand's square.
--- **Heightened (6th)** The hand has AC 27 and 60 Hit Points. When you Cast this Spell, you can choose to replace the hand's Manipulate or Attack option with Swat.
  • **Swat** The hand Flies to an enemy of your choice within range and attempts to [Push](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) that enemy, using your spell attack roll instead of an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to determine the results of the Push.
**Heightened (7th)** The hand has AC 30 and 70 Hit Points, and it is Large. When you Cast this Spell, you can choose to replace the hand's Manipulate or Attack option with Grasp or Swat.
  • **Grasp** The hand Flies to an enemy of your choice within range and attempts to [Grapple](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) that enemy, using your spell attack roll instead of an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to determine the results of the Grapple.
**Heightened (8th)** The hand has AC 32 and 80 Hit Points, and it is Large. When you Cast this Spell, you can choose to replace the hand's Manipulate or Attack option with Grasp, Punch, or Swat.
  • **Punch** The hand Flies to an enemy of your choice within range and attempts to Strike that enemy with crushing force. The hand's Strikes use your melee spell attack modifiers and deal 7d8 force damage. The hand's Strikes don't use or contribute to your multiple attack penalty.
**Heightened (9th)** The hand has AC 35 and 90 Hit Points, and it is Large. When you Cast this Spell, you can choose to replace the hand's Manipulate or Attack option with Crush, Grasp, Punch, or Swat.
  • **Crush** If the hand doesn't have a target grabbed, it Flies to an enemy of your choice within range and attempts to Strike that enemy with deadly crushing force. The hand's Strikes are melee spell attacks that deal 6d8 force damage. The hand's Strikes don't take or contribute to your multiple attack penalty. On a successful Strike, the target is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=20) by the hand until the end of your next turn, unless the hand moves or the target successfully [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) against your spell DC. However, if the hand has a target grabbed when it uses Crush, it instead [Constricts](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=10) the target, dealing 6d8 force damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save.
name: Forceful Hand

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Standard">
[Frigid Flurry](/Spells.aspx?ID=916)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cold" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=27" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 108

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Deity**
[Thremyr](/Deities.aspx?ID=497)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** line up to 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You place a palm to your lips and exhale a cold breath, whipping up a gust of wind that freezes the air's ambient moisture into a flurry of jagged shards. The flurry deals 9d6 cold damage and 9d6 slashing damage to all foes, with a basic Reflex save, but the gust flows harmlessly around your allies. The wind then picks you up and carries you to the other end of the area; though your movement within the gust can still trigger reactions, the storm of ice crystals prevents you from being affected by any effects, except for those that would [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the spell's magic or that the GM decides would affect snow.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The cold and slashing damage each increase by 1d6.
name: Frigid Flurry

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 108 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [Izuyaku](/Deities.aspx?ID=348) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 500 feet **Area** 10-foot radius, 50-foot-tall cylinder **Saving Throw** Reflex **Duration** 1 round --- A powerful geyser blasts up from the ground, potentially [pushing](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) creatures on top of it into the air. The bottom of this spell's area must be on solid ground. Each creature in the area takes 3d6 bludgeoning damage and 4d6 fire damage, with a Reflex save. A creature pushed into the air by the geyser can't be pushed beyond the top of the geyser. After being pushed into the air, a creature [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=402) unless it's flying or has some other means of staying aloft, taking falling damage (normally equal to half the distance it fell). After the geyser erupts, its area is filled with a cloud of steam for 1 round. All creatures in the steam are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4), and all creatures outside the steam are concealed to creatures within it. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and isn't pushed into the air.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is pushed 20 feet into the air.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage and is pushed 40 feet into the air. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6 bludgeoning and 1d6 fire.
name: Geyser

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 108 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Deity** [Olheon](/Deities.aspx?ID=479) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Area** 20-foot emanation centered on you **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- You're bathed in a smooth, almost glittering aura that improves the attitude of those near you. Any creature that ends its turn in the aura must attempt a Will saving throw with the following effects. No matter the result, it's then temporarily immune for 24 hours. The effect lasts until the spell ends, even after the creature leaves the aura. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and is aware of the aura.
**Success** The creature's attitude toward you improves by one step. If that improves its attitude to at least [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25), it can't take [hostile actions](/Rules.aspx?ID=300) against you, though the effect ends as soon as you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies.
**Failure** The creature's attitude toward you improves by two steps. It can't take hostile actions against you, though the effect ends as soon as you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies.
**Critical Failure** The creature's attitude becomes [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21) to you, though the effect ends as soon as you take a hostile action against the creature or its allies. While the creature is helpful, it can't take hostile actions against you.
name: Glimmer of Charm

You temporarily morph your limb into a clawed appendage. Make a melee spell attack roll against your target's AC. If you hit, you deal your choice of slashing or piercing damage equal to 1d6 plus your spellcasting ability modifier. On a critical success, you deal double damage plus 1d4 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d6 and the persistent bleed damage on a critical increases by 1d4.

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 109 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Deities** [Liisglan](/Deities.aspx?ID=540), [Telastmar](/Deities.aspx?ID=549) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Fortitude --- By suddenly altering gravity, you pull the target toward you. The target is [pulled](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 10 feet closer to you unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save. On a critical failure, it's also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). The effects of this spell change depending on the number of actions you spend when you Cast this Spell. **(somatic)** The spell targets one creature.
**(somatic, verbal)** The spell targets one creature and pulls the target 20 feet instead of 10.
**(material, somatic, verbal)** The spell targets up to 5 creatures.
name: Gravitational Pull

[Guiding Star](/Spells.aspx?ID=923)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=43" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 109

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** planetary

**Target** 1 creature you've met
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You call on the constellations of the night sky to guide a creature to the location where you Cast the Spell. Each time the target views the stars, it receives a mental nudge toward your chosen location, though it isn't compelled to follow. The target can recognize you as the source. If the creature goes to another planet or plane, the spell's effects are suppressed, but they resume if the creature returns.
name: Guiding Star

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Healing Plaster](/Spells.aspx?ID=925)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 109

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 5 feet

**Target** a handful of dirt or mud
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You purify some dirt or mud into a pliable, claylike plaster that can aid in clotting and healing. This restorative substance can be used in lieu of [healer's tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25) for [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) checks to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54) or [Treat Wounds](/Actions.aspx?ID=57). If the plaster isn't used within the spell's duration, or if you Cast the Spell again before using the plaster, it reverts to being normal non-magical earth.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The plaster grants anyone using it to Administer First Aid or Treat Wounds a +1 item bonus to the Medicine check.
name: Healing Plaster

<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 110

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You call forth a well of healing that you and your allies can draw from later. The well appears as a disc of shimmering light on the ground in a square within range. At any time during the duration, if you or an ally are adjacent to the well, that character can Interact to tap into the well's power, recovering 4d8 Hit Points. When the well has been tapped five times, the spell ends. The well is made of insubstantial energy, allowing creatures to move through it and even end their turn in its space.

 If you use an ability that increases the amount of healing this spell grants, that increase applies only to the first time someone taps the well.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The healing increases by 3.
name: Healing Well

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 110 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Range** varies **Target** 1 creature --- You gather magical energy into your palm, forming a concentrated ball of electricity that crackles and rumbles like impossibly distant thunder. Make a ranged spell attack roll against your target's AC. On a success, you deal 3d6 electricity damage. On a critical success, the target takes double damage and is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7) for 1 round. The number of actions you spend when Casting this Spell determines the range and other parameters. **(somatic, verbal)** This spell has a range of 30 feet.
**(material, somatic, verbal)** This spell has a range of 60 feet and deals half damage on a failure (but not a critical failure) as the electricity lashes out and jolts the target.
**Two Rounds** If you spend 3 actions Casting the Spell, you can avoid finishing the spell and spend another 3 actions on your next turn to empower the spell even further. If you do, after attacking the target, whether you hit or miss, the ball of lightning explodes, dealing 2d6 electricity damage to all other creatures in a 10-foot emanation around the target ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). Additionally, you spark with electricity for 1 minute, dealing 1 electricity damage to creatures that [Grab](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=18) you or that hit you with an [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) Strike or a non-[reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) melee weapon. --- **Heightened (+1)** The initial damage on a hit, as well as the burst damage for two-round casting time, each increase by 2d6, and the damage creatures take if they Grapple or hit you while you're in your sparking state increases by 1.
name: Horizon Thunder Sphere

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 110 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 living creature **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** 3 rounds --- You sift through myriad potential futures, seize upon one potential moment in which the target meets a particularly gruesome and fatal end, and then show it a vision of its impending demise. The intense mental vision grows more and more terrifying to the target as the doom grows closer, and it takes 3 rounds to reach its conclusion. The target must attempt a Will saving throw to determine the effects. At the end of the spell's duration, if the target was affected, the target witnesses its death and takes 6d6 mental damage. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is unaffected for 1 round. On the second round, it becomes [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16). Finally, on the third round, it becomes [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19). At the end of the third round, it takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature is immediately flat-footed. On the second round, it becomes frightened 2. Finally, on the third round, it also becomes [stunned 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36). At the end of the third round, the creature takes full damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature is immediately flat-footed and frightened 3. On the second round, it becomes stunned 1. Finally, on the third round, it also becomes [paralyzed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=28). At the end of the third round, the creature takes double damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 2d6.
name: Impending Doom

<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Inevitable Disaster](/Spells.aspx?ID=930)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Curse" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=38" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Prediction" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=132" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 111

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

You briefly glimpse cause and effect, putting into motion a chain of events that will visit doom on your enemy at some point in the future, though you're not quite sure when or how the doom will occur. Some kind of strange accident occurs 1d4 rounds later, dealing 55 damage to the creature, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. This happens regardless of where the target is located at the time, even if it travels to another plane. The GM should roll to determine when the damage occurs secretly and determine the form of the accident as well as the type of damage it deals; generally, the accident should be one that deals physical damage of some type (for instance, a tree branch might fall on the foe and deal bludgeoning damage, or a window might break and fling sharp slashing glass at them), though other damage types might be appropriate in more unusual environments.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 10.
name: Inevitable Disaster

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 111 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** up to 5 creatures **Saving Throw** Will --- You inflict your targets with a crushing doubt about everyone and everything around them. For the duration, each target is unwilling to receive any effects that require a willing target (in case the effect was secretly an attack), refuse to ingest anything (in case the substances have been replaced with poisons and cursed duplicates), doesn't treat anyone as an ally (in case its friends were replaced by an impostor), and doesn't believe anything it's told (in case of lies, though this doesn't mean the creature automatically believes the opposite either). It doesn't even fully trust its own ability to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26), as it becomes convinced it most likely remembered false information, as from a critical failure on a check to Recall Knowledge. Finally, the creature takes 6d6 mental damage at the end of each of its turns (with no save) unless it spent at least 1 action that turn [Seeking](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or otherwise interacting with its surroundings in an attempt to disbelieve the illusions that it feels are obviously surrounding it. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is affected for 1 round.
**Failure** The creature is affected for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The creature is affected for 1 hour.
name: Inexhaustible Cynicism

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Infectious Enthusiasm](/Spells.aspx?ID=932)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 112

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 round
</row>

</column>

---

With enchanted gusto, you encourage yourself to get things done and share your motivation with your allies. You gain a +1 status bonus to your choice of attack rolls, Will saves, or Charisma-based skill checks. During the spell's duration, an ally who observed you Casting this Spell and was within 30 feet when you did so can take a single action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to gain the same bonus you chose until the start of its next turn.

 Using this spell to influence someone in a social situation is typically considered a faux pas, negating the benefit on Charisma-based skills if the subject of the check observed you casting the spell.
name: Infectious Enthusiasm

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 112 **Tradition** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Deities** [Seramaydiel](/Deities.aspx?ID=483), [Winlas](/Deities.aspx?ID=656) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Area** 15-foot emanation **Target** enemies in the area **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** varies --- You call forth the chords of a tune so catchy that foes hearing it can't help but sing along, making it harder for them to communicate. Each target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** For 1 round, the creature can communicate only by singing along to the melody. It can speak only on its turn, and it must take the [Perform](/Actions.aspx?ID=59) action when it does. The result of the [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) check determines how well it sang, but it stills communicate the information regardless of the result, which has no further mechanical impact than the time spent to take the action. A single Perform check is sufficient for any amount of speaking on the creature's turn. This effect might interfere with verbal components of spells or other linguistic abilities that rely on precise speech; in order to use such an ability successfully, the creature must succeed at a DC 5 flat check. In addition, the creature takes a –2 status penalty on [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks, as singing its threats saps away some of the menace. The creature's constant humming of the tune also makes it nearly impossible for the creature to remain [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39).
**Failure** As success, but the duration is 3 rounds.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the duration is 1 hour.
name: Infectious Melody
