**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You spin illusions of flying shadows throughout the area. The shadows strike and cavort, harming your foes and blocking their vision with twisting shadow. Enemies in the area when you Cast the Spell take 6d8 bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage, with a basic Reflex or Will save, as shadows explode into being. You choose the damage type when you Cast the Spell, and each creature chooses the type of save it attempts before it rolls the save. The shadows then persist in the area for 1 minute, dealing 3d8 damage of the type you chose to any enemy that ends its turn in the area. All creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to enemies in the area, as the illusory shadows swarm over them and block light.

 A creature that spends an action to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) or otherwise interact with the _shadow raid_ can attempt a Perception check to [disbelieve](/Rules.aspx?ID=267) it. On a success, the creature halves the damage it takes from the shadows, and the shadows no longer impede its vision. These benefits last until the spell ends.

---
**Heightened (9th)** The initial damage increases by 2d8, and the damage to a creature that ends its turn in the area increases by 1d8.
name: Shadow Raid

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 129 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Deity** [Haggakal](/Deities.aspx?ID=493) **Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Trigger** You or another creature attacks a creature or fails at a [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5), [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6), or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) check. **Range** 30 feet **Target** the target of the triggering attack or skill check **Saving Throw** Will --- You alter the target's memories of the triggering event as they form. You choose another creature (which can be you) with the capacity to make the triggering attack or skill check, and you alter the target's memories to recall the creature you chose as responsible for the triggering attack or skill check. The target must attempt a Will save and is then temporarily immune for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target knows you attempted to alter its memories.
**Success** The target doesn't realize you attempted to alter its memories, though it knows you cast a spell.
**Failure** You successfully alter the target's memory. It isn't forced to react to the new memories in a particular way, and it's likely to question them if they contradict other information it knows or are implausible for the situation.
name: Shift Blame

By playing a brief tune on an instrument or in song, you cause the notes of the melody to physically manifest into a shimmering, translucent path. This path doesn't need to be over solid ground and can tilt up or down diagonally up to 45 degrees, but it must be a straight, 5-foot-wide line. It can support as many creatures as can physically fit on the bridge.

---

Blossoms grow from the ground in a small area, soothing away afflictions and persistent pains and harm. When any creature in that area rolls a successful save against a [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) or [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) effect, it gets a critical success instead. The blossoms grant assisted recovery to everyone in the area to end their [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), both when the spell is cast and at the start of each of your turns.

 Once the duration ends, the flowers lose their magical effect, but a few of them might survive in the area as long as they can naturally. This spell doesn't give a benefit on a save against an affliction unless the stage lasts 10 minutes or less.
name: Soothing Blossoms

[Soothing Spring](/Spells.aspx?ID=1000)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 129

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 1 minute ([material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284))

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 hour
</row>

</column>

---

Steam wisps into the air as a stone-lined pool appears in the ground and fills with hot water that bubbles up from the earth. The water is comfortably hot and smells mildly sulfurous in a not-unpleasant way. You are able to draw out the full energizing potential of the mineral-rich water. Any creature that spends the full hour soaking in the hot spring or basking in the mud from the bottom of the pit regains 10d8 Hit Points and feels refreshed, losing the [fatigued](/Conditions.aspx?ID=15) condition. As normal for effects that remove fatigue, this doesn't remove any underlying source of fatigue, such as lack of sleep, causing the condition to return if the underlying source isn't addressed.

 The pool is 20 feet on each side, and it's 5 feet deep. It can contain 16 Medium or smaller creatures at a time. A Large creature counts as eight Medium creatures and a Huge creature fills the entire pool. This pool has the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=153) trait and the same restrictions as magic items that create structures.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The healing increases by 2d8.
name: Soothing Spring

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Spiritual Attunement](/Spells.aspx?ID=1001)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 130

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You attune yourself to a spiritual plane of the Outer Sphere (see sidebar), connecting your spirit without fully transporting to it. Your Strikes and spells gain the alignment traits of the plane you chose, which deals additional damage to creatures with a weakness to either of those alignment traits. Furthermore, the plane protects you from energy of the alignments that oppose the plane. You gain resistance 5 to damage of the alignments that oppose those of your chosen plane. For example, if you chose [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17), your Strikes would gain the [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83) and [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99) traits, and you would gain resistance to chaotic and evil. If you choose the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15), the resistance is to negative and positive damage instead.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The resistance increases by 5.

<title level="3">The Outer Sphere</title> The nine main planes of the Outer Sphere are the homes of many deities, [celestials](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [monitors](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), and [fiends](/Traits.aspx?ID=70). Their names, along with their alignments, are: [Heaven](/Planes.aspx?ID=17) (LG), [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=20) (NG), [Elysium](/Planes.aspx?ID=16) (CG), [Axis](/Planes.aspx?ID=14) (LN), the [Boneyard](/Planes.aspx?ID=15) (N), the [Maelstrom](/Planes.aspx?ID=19) (CN), [Hell](/Planes.aspx?ID=18) (LE), [Abaddon](/Planes.aspx?ID=12) (NE), and the [Abyss](/Planes.aspx?ID=13) (CE).
name: Spiritual Attunement

Water blasts upward, coming out of the ground, rising from a pool, or even manifesting from thin air. Any creatures in the area take 2d4 bludgeoning damage, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex saving throw. A creature that critically fails its save is disoriented by the explosion of water, becoming off-guard until the end of your next turn.

 You can change this spell's area to a 5-foot burst, provided you center the burst in a body of water. This body of water can be as small as a pond or creek, but not as small as a puddle or bathtub.

---
**Heightened (+1)** The damage increases by 1d4.

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 130 **Tradition** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** unlimited --- You place a bit of knowledge deep in a creature's subconscious or make a willing creature forget some information until a specific trigger occurs. The target gets a Will saving throw to resist the effect. A willing creature can choose to fail its save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected and realizes you tried to alter its memory.
**Success** The target is unaffected but thinks your spell was something harmless instead of _sudden recollection_, unless it identifies the spell.
**Failure** You choose a piece of information the target didn't already know and create a trigger, such as seeing an object, hearing a key phrase, or witnessing an event. As soon as the target experiences the trigger, it receives the information and the spell ends. If the target was willing, you can instead choose a piece of information the target already knew and suppress its memory of that information until it experiences the trigger, after which it recovers the information and the spell ends.
name: Sudden Recollection

You conjure a chaotic celestial, monitor, or fiend to fight on your behalf. You summon a common chaotic creature that has the [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), or [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) trait and whose level is 5 or lower. You can't summon a creature unless its alignment is one of your deity's preferred alignments (or, if you don't have a deity, is within one step of your alignment). At the GM's discretion, some deities might restrict specific types of creatures, even if their alignments match. Heightening the spell increases the maximum level of creature you can summon.

---
**Heightened (6th)** Level 7.

**Heightened (7th)** Level 9.

**Heightened (8th)** Level 11.

**Heightened (9th)** Level 13.

**Heightened (10th)** Level 15.

<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Tradition**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

</row>

---

You briefly call forth the spirit of a legendary wizard from ages long past, which manifests as an immense humanoid figure (typically an [elf](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=2) or a [human](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=6)). The archmage occupies the space of a Large creature and has a Speed of 60 feet.

 **Arrive** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75)) _Archmage's Amplification_ The archmage arrives in a fierce burst of magical force, automatically dealing 3d4+3 force damage to up to 5 creatures it chooses within 100 feet of it. Like [_magic missiles_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180), this can be blocked by the [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=280) spell. The archmage also feeds magical power into some spells. Beneficial spells last longer due to the archmage's influence. When the remaining duration of a beneficial spell affecting you or one of your allies would decrease (usually at the start of the caster's turn), it doesn't decrease if the subject of the spell is within 100 feet of the archmage. This applies only if the spell's maximum duration was 3 rounds or longer. 

**Depart** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2)) _Archmage's Rebuke_ The archmage drains the magic from your foes. Each of your enemies within 100 feet of the archmage must attempt a Will save. On a failure, the creature is [stupefied 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 round, and if it's currently benefiting from any spells of a lower level than _summon archmage_, the archmage can choose any one of them and end the spell.
name: Summon Archmage

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 131

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure a lawful celestial, monitor, or fiend to fight on your behalf. You summon a common lawful creature that has the [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), or [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) trait and whose level is 5 or lower. You can't summon a creature unless its alignment is one of your deity's preferred alignments (or, if you don't have a deity, is within one step of your alignment). At the GM's discretion, some deities might restrict specific types of creatures, even if their alignments match. Heightening the spell increases the maximum level of creature you can summon.

---
**Heightened (6th)** Level 7.

**Heightened (7th)** Level 9.

**Heightened (8th)** Level 11.

**Heightened (9th)** Level 13.

**Heightened (10th)** Level 15.
name: Summon Axiom

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 131 **Tradition** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) **Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Requirements** You have a deity. **Range** 100 feet **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- Your faith is strong enough to briefly summon a direct representative of your deity, such as the deity's herald. The herald occupies the space of a Huge creature and has a Speed of 60 feet. The effects of this spell depend on your deity's alignment.
  • **Lawful Good**: **Arrive** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83), [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99)) _Aura of Retribution_ While within 100 feet of the herald, you and your allies gain the champion's [Retributive Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=5) reaction; **Depart** _Holy Smite_ The herald casts 5th-level [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (choosing good) in a 100-foot emanation.
  • **Neutral Good**: **Arrive** ([enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83)) _Aura of Redemption_ While within 100 feet of the herald, you and your allies gain the champion's [Glimpse of Redemption](/Actions.aspx?ID=6) reaction; **Depart** _Merciful Healing_ The herald casts the 3-action version of 5th-level [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) with a radius of 100 feet. This spell targets only you and your allies.
  • **Chaotic Good**: **Arrive** ([chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [good](/Traits.aspx?ID=83)) _Aura of Liberation_ While within 100 feet of the herald, you and your allies gain the champion's [Liberating Step](/Actions.aspx?ID=7) reaction; **Depart** _Radiating Freedom_ The herald affects you and all your allies within 100 feet with [_freedom of movement_](/Spells.aspx?ID=128). The duration is reduced to 3 rounds.
  • **Lawful Neutral**: **Arrive** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [lawful](/Traits.aspx?ID=99)) _Defy Chaos_ You and each ally within 100 feet of the herald gain resistance 20 against chaotic damage while the herald is present; **Depart** _Order's Wrath_ The herald casts 5th-level [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (choosing lawful) in a 100-foot emanation.
  • **Neutral**: **Arrive** ([transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) _Quickening Ripple_ You and each ally within 100 feet of the herald are [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) while the herald is present, and can use the additional action to Step, Stride, or Strike; **Depart** _Sapping Beam_ The herald casts 6th-level [_enervation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=687) in a 100-foot line.
  • **Chaotic Neutral**: **Arrive** ([abjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=2), [chaotic](/Traits.aspx?ID=25)) _Defy Law_ You and each ally within 100 feet of the herald gain resistance 20 against lawful damage while the herald is present; **Depart** _Chaos Hammer_ The herald casts 5th-level [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (choosing chaotic) in a 100-foot emanation.
  • **Lawful Evil**: **Arrive** _Aura of Iron_ While within 100 feet of the herald, you and your allies gain the champion's [Iron Command](/Actions.aspx?ID=552) reaction; **Depart** _Unholy Blight_ The herald casts 5th-level [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=86) (choosing evil) in a 100-foot emanation.
  • **Neutral Evil**: **Arrive** _Aura of Selfishness_ While within 100 feet of the herald, you and your allies gain the champion's [Selfish Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=553) reaction; **Depart** _Infinite Despair_ The herald casts 5th-level [_crushing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=57) in a 100-foot cone.
  • **Chaotic Evil**: **Arrive** _Aura of Vengeance_ While within 100 feet of the herald, you and your allies gain the champion's [Destructive Vengeance](/Actions.aspx?ID=554) reaction; **Depart** _Weapon Hurricane_ The herald casts 5th-level [_weapon storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=373) in a 100-foot cone, using d12 for the die size.
Herald Examples Each of the many deities of Golarion have their own herald; these are just a few of the most prominent.
  • **Lawful Good**: The Grand Defender ([Torag](/Deities.aspx?ID=18)), the Grim White Stag ([Erastil](/Deities.aspx?ID=6)), Hand of the Inheritor ([Iomedae](/Deities.aspx?ID=9))
  • **Neutral Good**: The Spirit of Adoration ([Shelyn](/Deities.aspx?ID=17)), Sunlord Thalachos ([Sarenrae](/Deities.aspx?ID=16))
  • **Chaotic Good**: Night Monarch ([Desna](/Deities.aspx?ID=5)), Thais ([Cayden Cailean](/Deities.aspx?ID=4))
  • **Lawful Neutral**: Lawgiver ([Abadar](/Deities.aspx?ID=1)), The Old Man ([Irori](/Deities.aspx?ID=10))
  • **Neutral**: Arcanotheign ([Nethys](/Deities.aspx?ID=12)), Personification of Fury ([Gozreh](/Deities.aspx?ID=8)), Steward of the Skein ([Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14))
  • **Chaotic Neutral**: The First Blade ([Gorum](/Deities.aspx?ID=7)), the Menotherian ([Calistria](/Deities.aspx?ID=3))
  • **Lawful Evil**: Basileus ([Asmodeus](/Deities.aspx?ID=2)), the Prince in Chains ([Zon-Kuthon](/Deities.aspx?ID=20))
  • **Neutral Evil**: Mother's Maw ([Urgathoa](/Deities.aspx?ID=19)), [the Stabbing Beast](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1007) ([Norgorber](/Deities.aspx?ID=13))
  • **Chaotic Evil**: Tarrasque ([Rovagug](/Deities.aspx?ID=15)), Yethazmari ([Lamashtu](/Deities.aspx?ID=11))
name: Summon Deific Herald

<title level="1" right="Spell 9" pfs="Standard">
[Summon Draconic Legion](/Spells.aspx?ID=1008)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 133

**Tradition**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 100 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

You momentarily summon an army of powerful dragons that unleash annihilating blasts as they descend upon your location. These dragons act as one and collectively occupy the space of a Gargantuan creature. They have a fly Speed of 100 feet. When you Cast this Spell, choose whether it summons [chromatic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=34) or  [metallic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=35) dragons.

 **Arrive** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) _Energy Annihilation_ The draconic legion breathes out two blasts of energy: a 120-foot line and a 60-foot cone that can't overlap. Each creature in either of the areas takes 10d8 damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex save). The dragons choose the damage type of each breath weapon, depending on the category of dragons summoned. Chromatic dragons can choose cold, fire, or poison for the cone and acid or electricity for the line; metallic dragons can choose cold or fire for the cone and acid, electricity, or fire for the line.

 **Depart** _Draconic Whirlwind_ The draconic legion strikes with fury, dealing 9d8 slashing damage (basic Reflex save) to all enemy creatures in its location or within 20 feet.
name: Summon Draconic Legion

<title level="1" right="Spell 10" pfs="Standard">
[Summon Kaiju](/Spells.aspx?ID=1009)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Incarnate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=388" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 133

**Tradition**
[Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 500 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until the end of your next turn
</row>

</column>

---

You briefly conjure a kaiju, a massive, rampaging monster with a unique name and legendary reputation. It rises from its secluded lair to annihilate your foes. The summoned kaiju occupies the space of a Gargantuan creature. When you Cast this Spell, choose one of the kaiju below to summon.

<title level="2">Secrets of Magic</title>

- **Agmazar the Star Titan** Speed 100 feet, climb 50 feet; **Arrive** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), [negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) _Channel Void_ Each foe in a 100-foot emanation takes 16d8 negative energy damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save; **Depart** ([transmutation](/Traits.aspx?ID=157)) _Gravitic Repulsion_ Each foe in a 50-foot emanation is [pushed](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) 100 feet away unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save.
- **Agyra, the Forever Storm** Speed 80 feet, fly 200 feet; **Arrive** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) _Breath of a Thousand Storms_ Agyra shoots two 1,200-foot lines of electricity, one from each of her heads. The lines can't overlap. Each creature along one of the lines takes 6d12 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save. On a failure, the creature is also [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round (or for 1 minute on a critical failure); **Depart** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) _Thunderous Blast_ Each creature within a 100-foot emanation takes 6d10 sonic damage with a basic Reflex save, and is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) on a failed save.
- **Bezravnis, the Inferno Below** Speed 100 feet, burrow 100 feet; **Arrive** ([earth](/Traits.aspx?ID=55), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) _Burrow from the Earth's Heart_ Bezravnis explodes from the ground where it was summoned, causing all creatures in a 50-foot emanation to take 8d12 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save. This potentially collapses structures with the collapse effect of the [_earthquake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=95) spell, except there is no chance of falling into a fissure; **Depart** _Web Hurricane_ Bezravnis departs with a hurricane of webbing. All creatures within a 50-foot emanation must succeed at a Reflex save or take a –20-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) (or is [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) until it escapes on a critical failure). The Escape DC is your spell DC.
- **Mogaru, the Final King** Speed 100 feet, swim 100 feet; **Arrive** ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) _Trance of the King's Melody_ Mogaru manifests with a burst of the song that summoned him, requiring all foes within 60 feet who can hear him to attempt a Will save, leaving them [stunned 3](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) on a failure; **Depart** ([evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72)) _Volcanic Breath_ Mogaru unleashes his scorching breath in a 120-foot cone. Each creature in the area takes 10d6 fire damage with a basic Reflex save. On a failure, it also takes 4d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
- **Vorgozen, the Shapeless Feeder** Speed 50 feet, climb 50 feet, swim 100 feet; **Arrive** ([necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117)) _Pollute Magic_ Vorgozen's foul presence pollutes magic around her. Each of your enemies within a 60-foot emanation that are under the effects of a spell must attempt a Fortitude save. On a failure, the creature is [sickened 2](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). Any of your enemies that Casts a Spell within 60 feet of Vorgozen must attempt a Fortitude save with the same effects unless it's already sickened; **Depart** ([acid](/Traits.aspx?ID=3), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) _Beam of Purest Vitriol_ Each creature in a 1,200-foot line takes 10d6 acid damage and 10d6 bludgeoning damage with a basic Fortitude save.
- **Yarthoon, the Moon Grub** Speed 60 feet, burrow 100 feet, fly 100 feet, swim 100 feet; **Arrive** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) _Algid Beam Barrage_ Yarthoon fires countless beams, targeting each of your enemies within 200 feet of it. The beams deal 10d6 cold damage with a basic Reflex save; **Depart** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=27), [conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=165)) _Frostbite Mist_ Chilling mist surges out in a 100-foot burst centered on Yarthoon and remains for 1 minute. This mist has the effects of [_obscuring mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=210), plus any creature within the area at the end of its turn take 5d6 cold damage with a basic Fortitude save.

<title level="2">Monsters of Myth</title>

- **Ebeshra, the Winged Razor** Speed 75 feet, fly 100 feet; **Arrive** ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=156)) _Planar Draw_ Each foe in a 100-foot emanation is pulled 50 feet towards the center, unless it succeeds at a Fortitude save. Creatures that fail and reach the center take 10d6 bludgeoning damage; **Depart** ([electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56), [evocation](/Traits.aspx?ID=65)) _Storm Discharge_ Each foe in a 240-foot line takes 12d8 electricity damage with a basic Reflex save
name: Summon Kaiju

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Summon Lesser Servitor](/Spells.aspx?ID=1010)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 134

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The summon lesser servitor spell cannot be used to summon imps._

**Tradition**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

While deities jealously guard their most powerful servants from the summoning spells of those who aren't steeped in the faith, this spell allows you to conjure an inhabitant of the [Outer Sphere](/Rules.aspx?ID=308) with or without the deity's permission. You summon a common [celestial](/Traits.aspx?ID=23), [monitor](/Traits.aspx?ID=111), or [fiend](/Traits.aspx?ID=70) whose level is –1 to fight on your behalf. Alternatively, you can choose a magical animal from the Outer Sphere as your lesser servitor. This animal is your choice of an [eagle](/Monsters.aspx?ID=172), [guard dog](/Monsters.aspx?ID=124), or [raven](/Monsters.aspx?ID=781). It gains the alignment matching your deity (or you, if you don't have a deity) and has the following trait depending on the alignment it gained: celestial for good, monitor for neutral, or fiend for evil.

 You can't summon a creature if it is opposed to your deity's alignment on either axis (or opposed to your alignment if you don't follow a deity). For example, if you deity is lawful good, you can't summon a chaotic or evil creature. The GM might determine that deities restrict specific types of creatures even if their alignments aren't diametrically opposed. For example, [Pharasma](/Deities.aspx?ID=14) would restrict the summoning of [sahkils](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=208).

 Heightening the spell increases the maximum level of creature you can summon and might add additional animal servitors, which otherwise work like the eagle, guard dog, and raven.

---
**Heightened (2nd)** Level 1.

**Heightened (3rd)** Level 2. You can summon a [black bear](/Monsters.aspx?ID=566), [giant bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=46), or [leopard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=67).

**Heightened (4th)** Level 3. You can summon a [great white shark](/Monsters.aspx?ID=365) or [tiger](/Monsters.aspx?ID=69).
name: Summon Lesser Servitor
